title: Echo Screens
desc: The dampening screens and bench braces are out again, which means the next debate will shake more than pride.

steps.text:
A chamber carved from resonant crystal will amplify anything given half a chance, including mistakes. To prevent every summit from escalating into a structural event, the outer wall carries a line of dampening screens made from layered resin cloth and the side benches sit on adjustable braces that keep them from rattling under the heavier vocal passages. Neither system is glamorous, which is why both are currently in need of attention.

The hall fitter gestures first at the wall and then at the bench line. "Rehang the dampening screens if you want the ladder work," she says. "Or tighten the bench braces if you prefer wedges and floor crawling. I would choose quickly. Scholars become thunderous when acoustics start flattering them."

options.label: Rehang the dampening screens along the outer wall
Tighten the bench braces beneath the side seating

steps.text:
The resin cloth screens hang in layered strips that absorb the harshest rebound without stealing the hall's useful resonance. Two have loosened at the top rail and another has slipped out of overlap, leaving a bright patch of crystal ready to throw every sharp note straight back into the ring. You climb, reset the hooks, and pull the strips into their proper stagger until the wall returns to muted order.

From the center of the chamber the difference is immediate. The hall still hums. It simply stops threatening to answer every raised voice with another raised voice of its own. The fitter claps once and listens to the shortened return. "Better," she says. "A hall may encourage debate. It need not applaud it."

options.label: Descend the ladder and leave the wall properly tempered

steps.text:
The side benches are meant for observers, note-carriers, and the unlucky. When their braces loosen, the seating picks up sympathetic vibration from the main ring and chatters at exactly the wrong moments. You crawl the line with a wedge key, tightening each brace shoe against the floor stone until the wood and crystal stop negotiating with one another and start behaving like furniture again.

The fitter tests the first bench with a hard shove, then another, and gets only the quiet solid answer she wanted. In a place like this, silence from the seating is a triumph. "Good," she says. "Now only the speakers will make fools of themselves audibly."

options.label: Pocket the wedge key and leave the benches steady
